title: Steffensia secunda
intro: Steffensia secunda, also known as "Vine of the Cross" or "Cruz de Tenayo", is a climbing plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Steffensia secunda include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Steffensia secunda is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used to make tinctures and salves, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
